title: рџ’» Copilot Code Impact [Edu] [v2]
description: Measures the volume and quality of Copilot-assisted code contributions including lines suggested, accepted, acceptance rate, and PR attribution.

panels.description: Data mode banner вЂ” shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN 'рџ“Ў Live Data вЂ” ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN 'рџЊ± Synthetic Seed Data вЂ” ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN 'рџЋ® Demo Environment вЂ” ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE 'вќ“ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# рџ’» Copilot Code Impact

**Supplementary Metrics вЂ” AI-Assisted Code Volume**

## What it Measures
This dashboard quantifies the volume and acceptance rate of code suggestions made by GitHub Copilot. It tracks lines suggested versus lines accepted across the 28-day window, and counts pull requests attributed to Copilot-active developers.

## Why it Matters
Acceptance rate and lines accepted are the primary signals of whether Copilot's suggestions are relevant and high-quality. Declining acceptance rates may indicate suggestion quality issues, mismatched context, or developers who have not yet learned to prompt effectively.

> вљ пёЏ **Attribution caveat**: A PR is counted as "Copilot-attributed" when the author has an active Copilot seat (activity within 28 days). This is a *proxy* вЂ” GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot telemetry. All Copilot attribution panels show this caveat.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| **Acceptance Rate** | в‰Ґ30% | Suggestions are relevant and trusted by developers |
| **Lines Accepted** | Growing over time | Copilot is contributing meaningfully to code output |
| **Copilot-Attributed PRs** | High % of total PRs | Active users are shipping regularly |

## How to Take Action
- **Low acceptance rate**: Review model context (open files, comments), ensure Copilot settings match language/framework in use
- **Declining lines accepted**: Check for reduced seat activity вЂ” may indicate disengagement or workflow changes
- **High suggestions, low acceptance**: Developers may be using Copilot for exploration but rejecting completions вЂ” investigate whether suggestions match coding standards

panels.description: Copilot Code Impact вЂ” lines accepted, acceptance rate, and attributed PRs.

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API в†’ loc_added_sum. 28-day window в‰  Grafana time picker. Org-level aggregate only.

panels.title: Lines Suggested (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API в†’ loc_suggested_to_add_sum. Org-level aggregate. Same NDJSON pattern.

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) в†’ download_links в†’ NDJSON. Metric: loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum.

panels.title: Copilot-Attributed PRs

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: вљ пёЏ PROXY: PR author has active Copilot seat. GitHub provides NO per-PR Copilot signal. Attribution is best-effort only.

options.content:
## рџ“– Lines Accepted (28d) вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` в†’ `loc_added_sum`

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Sum of loc_added_sum across daily rows in the selected date range from the org 28-day report.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **'Accepted' = kept at suggestion time** вЂ” developer can accept then immediately delete. Metric counts the accept event.
- **28-day rolling report** вЂ” covers last 28 days from today, not your Grafana time picker.
- **Org-level only** вЂ” no repo or team breakdown.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- How much accepted code is still in the codebase today.
- Which repositories or teams generated accepted lines.

panels.title: рџ“– Lines Accepted (28d) вЂ” Learning Guide

options.content:
## рџ“– Lines Suggested (28d) вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` в†’ `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Total lines Copilot suggested (accepted or not) in the selected date range.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **Same NDJSON pattern** вЂ” download_links в†’ fetch S3 URL в†’ parse NDJSON (same as all Copilot Metrics Reports endpoints).
- **Suggestion в‰  keystrokes saved** вЂ” loc_suggested counts lines in the suggestion block, not characters.
- **Org-level only** вЂ” no per-repository or per-team breakdown.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Which languages or editors generated the most suggestions.
- Suggestions partially accepted and then edited.

panels.title: рџ“– Lines Suggested (28d) вЂ” Learning Guide

options.content:
## рџ“– Acceptance Rate (%) вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) в†’ NDJSON в†’ `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Sum of accepted lines divided by sum of suggested lines across all language breakdowns in the org 28-day report.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **Multi-line suggestion bias** вЂ” accepting a 10-line suggestion counts 10 even if developer only kept 2.
- **NDJSON response pattern** вЂ” endpoint returns a `download_links` envelope, not inline JSON. Must fetch the signed S3 URL and parse line-by-line.
- **Org-level aggregate only** вЂ” no per-repo or per-team breakdown.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether accepted code was later reverted.
- Acceptance rate by team, repository, or language.

panels.title: рџ“– Acceptance Rate (%) вЂ” Learning Guide

options.content:
## рџ“– Copilot-Attributed PRs вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy вЂ” no per-PR Copilot signal)

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Count of merged PRs where PR author has an active Copilot seat. вљ пёЏ Proxy metric.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **вљ пёЏ Proxy metric** вЂ” GitHub provides no per-PR or per-commit signal indicating Copilot was used.
- **Active seat в‰  used on this PR** вЂ” author may have written PR entirely without Copilot.
- **Seat revocation retroactivity** вЂ” revoked seat removes 'Copilot-attributed' status on next sync.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- How much Copilot contributed to any specific PR.
- Code quality differences between attributed and non-attributed PRs.

panels.title: рџ“– Copilot-Attributed PRs вЂ” Learning Guide
Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Daily org totals from Copilot Metrics Reports API. Max 28-day range. Current day may lag by one sync.

panels.title: Acceptance Rate Trend

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Daily rate via JSONB unnesting of totals_by_language. Low-activity days в†’ extreme rate swings.

options.content:
## рџ“– Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` в†’ daily rows

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Time series showing both loc_suggested_to_add_sum and loc_added_sum per day.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **Rolling 28-day report boundary** вЂ” organization-28-day covers only the last 28 days; extending Grafana range shows no extra data.
- **No partial-day data** вЂ” each row is a complete calendar day; current day row may lag by one sync cycle.
- **Org-level daily totals** вЂ” spikes may be caused by one developer making many completions.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Day-of-week usage patterns.
- Per-user or per-language acceptance trends.

panels.title: рџ“– Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time вЂ” Learning Guide

options.content:
## рџ“– Acceptance Rate Trend вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API в†’ `totals_by_language` JSONB array в†’ `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Daily acceptance rate via jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_language) unnesting and dividing loc_added_sum by loc_suggested_to_add_sum.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **JSONB unnesting** вЂ” uses jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_language) to expand per-language array; more expensive than scalar columns.
- **totals_by_language vs totals_by_editor** вЂ” report has both arrays; rates should match but may differ slightly.
- **Day-to-day volatility on low-activity days** вЂ” few suggestions on a quiet day can swing rate from 0% to 100%.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Acceptance rate for a specific language or editor.
- Whether changes are driven by usage pattern shifts or suggestion quality.

panels.title: рџ“– Acceptance Rate Trend вЂ” Learning Guide
Copilot PR Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: вљ пёЏ PROXY: PR author has active Copilot seat. GitHub provides NO per-PR Copilot usage signal. All вљ пёЏ panels use this proxy.

panels.title: Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10

panels.description: Source: users-28-day/latest (SEPARATE endpoint from org report). Per-user data вЂ” consider privacy before sharing.

options.content:
## рџ“– Copilot PR Rate (%) вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy вЂ” no per-PR Copilot signal exists)

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Percentage of merged PRs whose author has an active Copilot seat. вљ пёЏ Proxy вЂ” GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot usage signal.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **Proxy, not direct measurement** вЂ” active seat is the only available signal; developer may have written this PR without Copilot.
- **Active seat в‰  used on this PR** вЂ” seat active within 28 days anywhere, not on this specific PR.
- **Seat revocation retroactivity** вЂ” revoked seat removes attribution on next sync.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- How much Copilot contributed to any specific PR.
- Code quality differences between attributed and non-attributed PRs.

panels.title: рџ“– Copilot PR Rate (%) вЂ” Learning Guide

options.content:
## рџ“– Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users) вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) в†’ per-user NDJSON

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_user_metrics`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Top 10 users by total loc_added_sum from the per-user 28-day report вЂ” a SEPARATE endpoint from the org report.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **Separate API endpoint** вЂ” uses users-28-day/latest, NOT organization-28-day/latest. Same download_links в†’ NDJSON pattern; per-user report has one row per user per day.
- **Privacy consideration** вЂ” per-user line counts visible to org owners and billing managers.
- **Lines added в‰  productivity** вЂ” auto-completing import statements ranks highly without writing complex code.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Code complexity or quality of accepted lines.
- Whether high line counts correlate with faster delivery or fewer bugs.

panels.title: рџ“– Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users) вЂ” Learning Guide
Copilot Seats Loaded

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.description: DATA HEALTH: row count of copilot_seats. If 0, check sync logs for a silent 403 on GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats.

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: sync_jobs. 'success' status does NOT guarantee all resources synced вЂ” a silent 403 on any fetcher still produces status='success'.

options.content:
## рџ“– Copilot Seats Loaded вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source [в†’ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
Row count of copilot_seats table вЂ” a data health indicator. If 0, the last sync did not load seat data.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **Silent 403 failure** вЂ” sync server logs warning on 403 but continues; table stays at 0. 'Success' sync в‰  seats loaded.
- **TRUNCATE + INSERT every sync** вЂ” unlike PRs/deployments (incremental), copilot_seats fully replaced each sync.
- **Proxy for sync health** вЂ” use alongside 'Last Successful Sync'; check server logs if this shows 0.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether seat data is accurate or stale.
- Breakdown by team or repository.

panels.title: рџ“– Copilot Seats Loaded вЂ” Learning Guide

options.content:
## рџ“– Last Successful Sync вЂ” Learning Guide

### рџ“Ў GitHub API Source
Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API)

### рџ—„пёЏ Database Table(s)
`sync_jobs`

### рџ“ђ How It's Calculated
MAX(finished_at) from sync_jobs where status='success'. Shows when the last complete sync run finished.

### рџ”Ќ SQL Query
```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```

### вљ пёЏ Calculation Challenges
- **'Successful' sync в‰  all data loaded** вЂ” job completes with 'success' while individual fetchers (e.g., Copilot seats) fail silently. Always check records_synced per resource.
- **Timestamps in UTC** вЂ” finished_at stored in UTC; Grafana displays in browser timezone.
- **sync_jobs is append-only** вЂ” each sync adds a row; table grows indefinitely.

### рџ’Ў What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether all resources were fetched (check records_synced column).
- Failure reason if last job has non-'success' status.

panels.title: рџ“– Last Successful Sync вЂ” Learning Guide
